figures.caption: Figure 1: Overview of the L-DPS framework. The VAE is trained to reconstruct the parameter field κ(x) with the encoder Eψ and decoder Dψ; the latent diffusion prior ϵω(zt, t) is trained on the encoded latents z0; and the neural surrogate Fϕ (FNO / DeepONet / ViT) is trained to approximate the forward PDE solver F on the full pressure field û(x). At inference, the reverse diffusion process (bottom) starts from zT , repeatedly computes the Tweedie estimate ẑ0, decodes to κ̂(x), evaluates the data-misfit Ldata against the sparse observations d, and applies the noise-adaptive guidance zt−1 = z′t−1 − ζ(η, γ) gt/∥gt∥ until t = 0; the final decoded field κ̂(x) is the estimate.

Figure 2: Comparison of the reference parameter field (yref ) and the fields estimated with the L-DPS+FNO (ŷL−DPS), full-space DPS (ŷDPS), CLDM (ŷCLDM ), and InvFNO (ŷInvFNO) methods. Also shown are the reference u solution (uref ) and the point errors between estimated y and yref . The inverse solutions are obtained under three settings (different rows): (a) The number of observations Nobs = 64 and the relative noise level γ = 0.1, (b) Nobs = 128 and γ = 0.01, and (c) Nobs = 128 and γ = 0.05. CLDM point errors are plotted with a different color scale than the other three methods because they have a significantly smaller range.

Figure 3: Relative ℓ2 reconstruction error on y = log κ versus number of sensors Nobs across four noise levels γ ∈ {0, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1} (one panel each). L-DPS+FNO consistently matches or beats the baselines across the grid; the gap widens in the sparse/noisy regime, where amortized methods (CLDM, InvFNO) and full-space DPS degrade noticeably.

Figure 4: VAE reconstruction quality for three priors. Rows: single-scale Gaussian, multi-scale Gaussian, and binary non-Gaussian. Columns: reference κ, VAE reconstruction, and the reconstruction error. Each row shows the medianerror test sample.
Figure 5: Distribution of the VAE reconstruction relative ℓ2 error over 500 test samples for each prior. The three distributions are concentrated well below 4%.

Figure 7: Inverse relative ℓ2 errors versus the number of observations Nobs for six guidance strategies across four noise levels γ ∈ {0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.1}. All methods share the same L-DPS+FNO pipeline and differ only in the guidance rule.

Figure 8: Inverse reconstructions at Nobs = 128, γ = 0.01 for three priors. Rows from top to bottom: Single-scale Gaussian, multi-scale Gaussian, Non-Gaussian binary. Columns from left to right: reference yref , L-DPS+FNO estimate ŷL−DPS , and point errors ŷL−DPS−yref . Solutions are shown for the test cases corresponding to the median ℓ2 errors.

Figure 9: Inverse reconstructions at Nobs = 128, γ = 0.01 from a single L-DPS+FNO model trained on a mixed dataset. Rows correspond to the three source priors; the model is not told which prior a test sample came from.
Table 7: Latent UNet diffusion prior hyperparameters.
